revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24699703697|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{Expand Japanese|羽咋神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Hakui Shrine | native_name = 羽咋神社 | image = Hakui-jinja haiden.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Hakui Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|36.897235|136.779559}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Prince Iwatsukuwake|lt=Prince Iwatsukuwake|ja|磐衝別命|qid=Q11587884|1=Iwatsukiwake no Mikoto}} }} {{nihongo|'''Hakui Shrine'''|羽咋神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Noto Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11612019|ltq=Q11612019|qid=Q11612019|1=List of Shikinaisha in Noto Province}} ** Series ordinal: 5 ** P155: {{ill|Kanshiro Shrine|lt=Kanshiro Shrine|qid=Q135040302|1=Kanshiro Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135040303|ltq=Q135040303|qid=Q135040303|1=Setohikono Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Hakui, Ishikawa|lt=Hakui|ar|هاكوي (إيشيكاوا)|arz|هاكوى, ايشيكاوا|azb|هاکویی|ceb|Hakui (lungsod)|ce|Хакуй (гӀала)|de|Hakui|en|Hakui, Ishikawa|envoyage|Hakui|es|Hakui|fa|هاکویی|fi|Hakui|fr|Hakui|gl|Hakui|it|Hakui|ja|羽咋市|ko|하쿠이시|mg|Hakui|ms|Hakui, Ishikawa|mzn|هاکویی، ایشیکاوا|new|हकुई|nl|Hakui|pl|Hakui|pt|Hakui|ro|Hakui|ru|Хакуй (город)|sv|Hakui|tg|Ҳакуи|tl|Hakui, Ishikawa|tr|Hakui|tt|Хакуй (шәһәр)|uk|Хакуй (Ісікава)|vi|Hakui, Ishikawa|war|Hakui, Ishikawa|zh_classical|羽咋市|zh_min_nan|Hakui-chhī|zh_yue|羽咋|zh|羽咋市|zhvoyage|羽咋|qid=Q822585|1=Hakui, Ishikawa}} * {{ill|Q1047671|ltq=Q1047671|qid=Q1047671|1=Noto Province}} * {{ill|Hakui District, Ishikawa|lt=Hakui district|ceb|Hakui Gun|en|Hakui District, Ishikawa|es|Distrito de Hakui (Ishikawa)|fa|شهرستان هاکوی، ایشیکاوا|fr|District de Hakui|id|Distrik Hakui, Ishikawa|it|Distretto di Hakui|ja|羽咋郡|ko|하쿠이군|pl|Powiat Hakui|uk|Повіт Хакуй|zh|羽咋郡|qid=Q1130953|1=Hakui District, Ishikawa}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|36.897235|136.779559}} == image (P18) == * Hakui-jinja haiden.JPG == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Prince Iwatsukuwake|lt=Prince Iwatsukuwake|ja|磐衝別命|qid=Q11587884|1=Iwatsukiwake no Mikoto}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 182347 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|1=Ken-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 9220005005542 == Commons category (P373) == * Hakui-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '石川県羽咋市川原町エ164', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Hakui-jinja}} [[JA:羽咋神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11610064}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Hakui Shrine |画像 = [[File:Hakui-jinja haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|qid=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hakui|Q822585|qid=Q822585|1=Hakui, Ishikawa}} Kawara-machi E164 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Iwatsukuwake-no-Mikoto|Q11587884|qid=Q11587884|lt=Ishitsukuwake-no-Mikoto|1=Iwatsukiwake no Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with copper-plate roofing |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=September 25|Q2886|qid=Q2886|1=September 25}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ishikawa|label=Hakui Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Hakui-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Hakui Shrine''' (はくいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kawara-machi, {{ill|lt=Hakui|Q822585|qid=Q822585|1=Hakui, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|qid=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} and a former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are the following four pillars: ; Principal deity :* '''{{ill|lt=Iwatsukuwake-no-Mikoto|Q11587884|qid=Q11587884|lt=Ishitsukuwake-no-Mikoto|1=Iwatsukiwake no Mikoto}}''' (いわつくわけのみこと) :*: Also written "Iwatsukuwake-no-Mikoto" in some sources. The tenth {{ill|lt=imperial prince|Q12886381|qid=Q12886381|1=Imperial Prince}} of the 11th Emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|qid=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}. Regarded as the ancestor of the Hakui no Kimi. ; Associate deities :* Iwakiwake-no-Mikoto (いわきわけのみこと) :*: Son of Ishitsukuwake-no-Mikoto. Said to have been appointed Hakui no Kuni-no-Miyatsuko'Kokuzo Hongi' (volume 10 of the '{{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|qid=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}}'), Hakui Kokuzo entry.. :* Otokarihatatobe-no-Mikoto (おとかりはたとべのみこと) :*: Consort of Emperor Suinin and mother of Ishitsukuwake-no-Mikoto. :* Chigaeshi-no-Ōkami (ちがえしのおおかみ) :*: Merged during the Meiji era. === History === The founding date is unknown. {{ill|lt=Iwatsukuwake-no-Mikoto|Q11587884|qid=Q11587884|lt=Ishitsukuwake-no-Mikoto|1=Iwatsukiwake no Mikoto}} was dispatched by order of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|qid=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}} when a monstrous bird appeared in this area tormenting the people. He slew the bird and promoted agriculture in the area. Later, the Hakui no Kimi clan, who governed the area, enshrined their ancestor Ishitsukuwake-no-Mikoto as their {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|qid=Q767655|1=Ujigami}}, which is said to be the shrine's origin. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, completed in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}), it is listed as "Hakui Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Hakui District|Q1130953|qid=Q1130953|1=Hakui District, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Noto Province|Q1047671|qid=Q1047671|1=Noto Province}}, and was ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}. The Hachiman Shrine located about 200 meters to the southeast was the original site of Hakui Shrine (or alternatively, the site of Ishitsukuwake-no-Mikoto's residence). During the {{ill|lt=Bunmei|Q1193544|qid=Q1193544|1=Bunmei}} era ({{ill|lt=1469|Q6566|qid=Q6566|1=1469}}–{{ill|lt=1487|Q6652|qid=Q6652|1=1487}}), shrine buildings were constructed atop the Ōtsuka kofun mound and the deity was transferred there. Another theory states that initially only a pine tree stood atop the Ōtsuka mound with no buildings constructed, and after the pine was destroyed by lightning during the {{ill|lt=Hōreki|Q1194397|qid=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} era ({{ill|lt=1751|Q7615|qid=Q7615|1=1751}}–{{ill|lt=1764|Q7659|qid=Q7659|1=1764}}), a treasure hall was built and made the main hall during the {{ill|lt=Bunka|Q1373146|qid=Q1373146|1=Bunka}} era ({{ill|lt=1804|Q6894|qid=Q6894|1=1804}}–{{ill|lt=1818|Q6964|qid=Q6964|1=1818}}). In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, and in Meiji 14 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|qid=Q7808|1=1881}}) it was elevated to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}. In Meiji 42 ({{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|qid=Q2057|1=1909}}), the deity Chigaeshi-no-Ōkami was transferred and merged from the Sanbyōkari Shrine atop the Hime-zuka (one of the Hakui Seven Mounds), which stood on the planned route of the {{ill|lt=Nanao Line|Q5357471|qid=Q5357471|1=Nanao Line}} railway. In {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|qid=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1917|Q2092|qid=Q2092|1=1917}}), when the Ōtsuka was designated as a candidate imperial mausoleum, the shrine buildings were moved to the current location at the southern foot of the Ōtsuka's rear circular portion. === Precinct === * Honden (main hall) — three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with copper-plate roofing. * Heiden * Haiden (worship hall) — built in {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|qid=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1832|Q7584|qid=Q7584|1=1832}}), five-bay {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|qid=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} with copper-plate roofing. * Water of Goryō-yama — spring water from Mount Goryō. * Ōtsuka (Goryō-yama) — one of the {{ill|lt=Hakui Seven Mounds|Q11610061|qid=Q11610061|1=Hakui-nanatsuka}}. A {{ill|lt=keyhole-shaped kofun|Q11268718|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Tomb of Iwatsukuwake-no-Mikoto." It remains behind the shrine buildings. * Ōtani-zuka (Ōji-zuka, Kannon-zuka) — one of the Hakui Seven Mounds. A {{ill|lt=round kofun|Q11394747|qid=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Tomb of Iwaki-wake-no-Ō." It remains to the right of the shrine buildings. File:Ootsuka (Hakui-nanatsuka).JPG|Ōtsuka (Goryō-yama) File:Ootani-zuka (Hakui-nanatsuka).JPG|Ōtani-zuka (Ōji-zuka, Kannon-zuka) === Auxiliary shrines === All are extra-precinct branch shrines located within Hakui City'Ishikawa-ken no Chimei,' Hakui Shrine entry.. * Hachiman Shrine (Kawara-machi, {{ウィキ座標|36|53|45.75|N|136|46|56.48|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|location|name=Hachiman Shrine (former main shrine)}}) *: Either the former shrine site or the site of Ishitsukuwake-no-Mikoto's residence. Originally located in front of {{ill|lt=Hakui Station|Q5640629|qid=Q5640629|1=Hakui Station}} but relocated to the current site in {{ill|lt=1960|Q3754|qid=Q3754|1=1960}} (Shōwa 35)[https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/677976 Enshrined deity to temporary residence — Hachiman Shrine in Hakui] Chūnichi Shimbun, accessed April 24, 2023.. As it was on the planned route of an urban planning road, in {{ill|lt=2023|Q49622|qid=Q49622|1=2023}} (Reiwa 5) the building was moved about 10 meters backward by hikiya (building-moving) construction; in April a temporary transfer ceremony was held, and in July a formal enshrinement ceremony returned the deity to the building. * Sukunahikona Shrine (Kawara-machi) *: Enshrined atop the Mizuinu-zuka (one of the Hakui Seven Mounds). * Inari Shrine (Asahi-machi) * Inari Shrine (Hon-machi) * Uotori Shrine (うおとりじんじゃ, Shimade-machi) * Kotohira Shrine (ことひらじんじゃ, Ōkawa-machi) === Related sites === * Tsurugi-zuka ({{Coord|36|53|45.67|N|136|46|59.51|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark|name=Tsurugi-zuka (former site of Hachiman Shrine)}}) *: The former site of the extra-precinct Hachiman Shrine; said to be either the estate site of Ishitsukuwake-no-Mikoto or the original Hakui Shrine. === Major festivals === * Kawawatashi Shinji (Saturday and Sunday in mid-September) *: The mikoshi crosses the Chōja River to go to Hachiman Shrine. It is also said that Ishitsukuwake-no-Mikoto visits the female deity of Hachiman Shrine. * Annual festival, Karadoyama Shinji Sumō ({{ill|lt=September 25|Q2886|qid=Q2886|1=September 25}}) *: In honor of the enshrined deity Ishitsukuwake-no-Mikoto, who was said to love sumo, this event has been held since the {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|qid=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} era ({{ill|lt=1394|Q6398|qid=Q6398|1=1394}}–{{ill|lt=1428|Q6315|qid=Q6315|1=1428}}). {{ill|lt=September 25|Q2886|qid=Q2886|1=September 25}} is said to be the date of the deity's death. === Cultural properties === === Ishikawa Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Intangible folk cultural property[http://www2.city.hakui.ishikawa.jp/rekimin/index.php?action=pages_view_main&page_id=76 Cultural properties of Hakui City] (Hakui City Museum of History and Folklore). ** Karadoyama Shinji Sumō — designated as a Hakui City intangible folk cultural property on May 25, 2001 (Heisei 13). Redesignated as an Ishikawa Prefecture intangible folk cultural property on February 3, 2015 (Heisei 27) (city designation was rescinded). === Hakui City designated cultural properties === * Natural monuments ** Zelkova tree of Hakui Shrine — height 20.0 meters, estimated age 600 years. Designated January 11, 1972 (Shōwa 47). ** Sawara cypress of Hakui Shrine — height 21.0 meters, estimated age over 250 years. Designated January 11, 1972 (Shōwa 47). === Visitor information === '''Address''' * {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|qid=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hakui|Q822585|qid=Q822585|1=Hakui, Ishikawa}} Kawara-machi E164 '''Access''' * Rail: {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|qid=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) {{ill|lt=Nanao Line|Q5357471|qid=Q5357471|1=Nanao Line}} {{ill|lt=Hakui Station|Q5640629|qid=Q5640629|1=Hakui Station}} (approx. 5-minute walk) '''Nearby''' * {{ill|lt=Hakui Seven Mounds|Q11610061|qid=Q11610061|1=Hakui-nanatsuka}} * Karadoyama Sumo Arena — one of Japan's three great outdoor sumo arenasThe other two are Haji Shrine in {{ill|lt=Fujioka|Q596486|qid=Q596486|1=Fujioka, Gunma}}, {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|qid=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, and {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|qid=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|qid=Q35765|1=Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}.. The bowl-shaped depression forming the arena is said to be the excavation trace from constructing the Ōtsuka mound. * Honnen-ji Temple === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * 'Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Ishikawa-ken no Chimei' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Hakui City, Hakui Shrine entry === External links === {{Commonscat|Hakui-jinja}} * [http://www.city.hakui.lg.jp/sypher/www/section/detail.jsp?id=591 Hakui Shrine] — Ishikawa Prefecture website {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Hakui Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hakui Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Noto Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Noto Province]] [[Category:石川県の旧県社]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:羽咋市の建築物]] [[Category:Municipal History of Hakui]]
